text: The present paper reports on the outcomes of the ParlaMint II project. Specifically, it describes the multi-language corpora of parliamentary debates of 29 European countries and autonomous regions obtained through the project, including the coverage (at least for the period between 2015 and 2022) and size (more than one billion words) of the corpora, their uniform coding and annotation (including metadata about 24,000 speakers), and the linguistic annotation up to the level of Universal Dependencies syntax and named entities. This paper specifically concentrates on the improvements over the ParlaMint I outcome and on the processes applied for the creation of the corpora. It starts by giving some information about the encoding infrastructure used, about the application of GitHub for corpus development, about the procedures followed to prepare the individual corpora and the common processing applied to all of them in order to produce their distribution, and about the CLARIN infrastructure used for their dissemination. Then, it continues by giving some numbers about the produced resources, and describing the improvements and additions that were realises within the context of ParlaMint II, such as metadata localisation, new metadata (for some countries and parties) metadata, machine translation of the corpora to English and the corresponding semantic tagging, and, as a pilot, the production of speech corpora. Finally, it gives some information on the dissemination and outreach activities carried out and the planned future work.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
